response.justification: The candidate is an experienced, versatile engineer with strong craftsmanship, but he does not meet the mandatory requirements for this role. His professional C++ experience is limited to roughly seven months at National Instruments (2014-2015) plus a thesis project, far short of the required 5-10 years of hands-on Windows C++ development. His database background is MySQL and MongoDB rather than SQL Server or Oracle, and his financial exposure (RIDE Capital, a retail investment platform) does not constitute fixed income, capital markets, or investment banking domain expertise. His career centers on web, full-stack, and DLang systems work, which does not transfer to this specialized C++ capital-markets consulting engagement.
strengths: 15+ years of broad software engineering experience across web, mobile, desktop, and embedded systems
Proven low-latency, high-throughput systems work (DLang microservices handling millions of requests per second)
Strong engineering practices: TDD, CI/CD, DevOps tooling, and open-source contributions
concerns: Only about seven months of professional C++ experience, versus the mandatory 5-10 years on Windows
No SQL Server or Oracle experience; database background is MySQL and MongoDB
No fixed income, capital markets, or investment banking domain knowledge

key_factors.factor: Missing capital markets / fixed income domain expertise
Strong general engineering and performance-systems background
